issue_title: Links to posts from remote servers not loaded in the local web-app
issue_body:
### Detailed description

When you click on a link to a post on a remote server, a new web-browser tab is opened showing the post on the remote server. This is confusing, because when you open the same post from a timeline, it opens on your own server in your local web-app. 

The changes in Mastodon 4 have unified the local dynamic and static pages, and that is a good thing. But opening those links (in posts) in a new tab and bringing the user unexpectedly to a remote server, still confuses people. They try to interact with the post and stumble on unnecessary barriers. There are even people who try to sign-in to that remote server (because a sign-in button is displayed) or worse (trying to sign-up on the remote server). 

It shouldn't be that difficult to fetch the link, like it is done when pasting it in the search field. The problem about if it's really a link to a Mastodon or other fediverse post can be solved before the post is parsed. AFAIK this is also done with mentions (if @user@server.tld is non existing, it displays as plain text).

### Specifications

Mastodon 4.0.2
pr_title: Change links to posts and profiles in posts to open in web UI
pr_body:
One of the most common reasons for people landing outside their logged-in Mastodon interface now is links to posts within other posts, made more popular by quoting features in various mobile apps. Mobile apps like Tusky and Toot! take advantage of the search API to check all tapped links in posts for potentially being posts or profiles, and if so, opening them in-app. In the browser, we are more limited: we can't run an asynchronous request to the search API in the link click event *and* open a link in a new tab if it's just a regular link, as this triggers browsers' pop-up blocking; you can generally only open new tabs only within immediate the lifespan of the click event. This is why instead I am introducing a server-side redirect route that will perform the check and redirect. Client-side we only need to replace all links to go through this route, instead of going directly to the target.

___

Fixes MAS-202, fixes #1399, fixes #21869
